message:
perf(vdbe): Arc<str>/Arc<[u8]> adaptation, binary-search upsert, SmallVec bindings, metrics elision

Multi-pronged performance improvements to the VDBE engine:

Arc<str>/Arc<[u8]> adaptation:
- write_text_to_reg/write_blob_to_reg simplified: buffer-reuse match
  arms removed since Arc is immutable — direct assignment replaces
  clear+push_str/extend_from_slice patterns
- cursor_column cache writes simplified similarly
- String8/String/Blob opcode fallbacks use Arc::from("") / Arc::from([])
- Concat opcode builds String then converts via .into()
- sql_cast, coerce_for_comparison, record_blob_bytes, sorter data
  all adapted for Arc types

MemDatabase::upsert_row O(log n) lookup:
- Replaces linear .iter().find() with binary_search_by_key() since
  rows are maintained in rowid-sorted order

SmallVec<[SqliteValue; 8]> for bindings:
- Most prepared statements have <8 parameters; keeping them inline
  avoids a heap allocation per statement execution
- New set_bindings_slice() method for clone-from-slice without
  intermediate Vec

Metrics elision:
- vdbe_metrics_enabled() result latched into self.collect_vdbe_metrics
  once per execute() call instead of re-reading the atomic on every
  opcode/column/record-decode
- decode_record renamed to decode_record_with_metrics with explicit
  bool parameter; test-only wrapper preserves old API
- local_opcode_execution_totals allocated only when metrics are enabled
  (Option<Box<[u64]>> instead of always-allocated stack array)
- Instant::now() call gated behind needs_statement_timing flag
- Post-execution bookkeeping early-returns when timing is disabled

Vectorized scan/append:
- vectorized.rs: data.extend(bytes) → data.extend_from_slice(bytes)
- vectorized_scan.rs: column_value_at uses .into() for Arc conversion

New tests:
- test_set_bindings_slice_keeps_small_binding_sets_inline
- test_decode_record_with_metrics_flag_bypasses_global_reload
- test_swiss_index_metrics_emitted_on_cursor_ops simplified to test
  functional correctness only (probe metrics are trace-level gated)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
